summary: Frontend translation testing for Arabic to English. Found multiple untranslated Arabic texts in 3 pages: WarehouseTransfers.js, BranchOrders.js, and Reservations.js. HR and Delivery pages are fully translated.

untranslated_texts: Status labels: تم الاستلام, تم الطلب, ملغي (lines 200-202)
Priority labels: عاجل, مرتفع, عادي, منخفض (lines 214-217)
طلب جديد من قسم التصنيع - dialog title (line 484)
Status labels: قيد الانتظار, تمت الموافقة, تم الشحن, تم التسليم, ملغي (lines 212-218)
إنشاء حجز جديد - button (visible in UI)
translation_issues.note: These translations exist in autoTranslate.js but may not be applied correctly

untranslated_texts: أصناف تحتاج إعادة تعبئة - low stock text
translation_issues.note: Some translations exist but not applied

action_items: 1. WarehouseTransfers.js: Wrap all hardcoded Arabic texts with t() function - approximately 20+ texts need wrapping
2. BranchOrders.js: Wrap all hardcoded Arabic texts with t() function - approximately 15+ texts need wrapping

3. Add missing translations to autoTranslate.js for: إدارة المخزون والتحويلات, تحويلات المخزون وطلبات الشراء, تحويلات معلقة, قيد التوصيل, طلبات شراء معلقة, تحويلات مكتملة, تحويل جديد, مخزون التصنيع, أصناف تحتاج إعادة تعبئة
4. Verify Reservations.js translations are being applied correctly
critical_code_review_comments: WarehouseTransfers.js has many hardcoded Arabic strings not wrapped with t() function
BranchOrders.js has many hardcoded Arabic strings not wrapped with t() function
Status labels in getStatusBadge() and getStatusLabel() functions return hardcoded Arabic
Priority labels in getPriorityBadge() function return hardcoded Arabic

success_rate.frontend: 60% - HR and Delivery pages fully translated, WarehouseTransfers, BranchOrders, and parts of WarehouseManufacturing need fixes

context_for_next_testing_agent: Translation testing for Arabic to English. Pages tested: /warehouse, /warehouse-manufacturing, /delivery, /reservations, /hr, /branch-orders. HR and Delivery are fully translated. WarehouseTransfers.js and BranchOrders.js have many hardcoded Arabic texts that need to be wrapped with t() function.
